join_order.notes: Use customer_segment_postings to enumerate BUILDING customer rowids, load c_custkey, and expand only matching customers through orders_cust_postings instead of scanning all 15M orders.

join_order.build: orders(filtered by customer join and o_orderdate < 1995-03-15, sorted by o_orderkey)
join_order.probe: lineitem(via lineitem_order_postings in ascending order key)
join_order.notes: Materialize a compact qualifying-order array of {o_orderkey,o_orderdate,o_shippriority}, sort it by o_orderkey, then walk lineitem postings in that order so rowid and payload accesses become streaming across lineitem.

scan.table: customer(filtered via postings) -> orders(postings expansion) -> qualifying_orders(sorted by o_orderkey) -> lineitem(postings expansion)
scan.access_pattern: selective index expansion on customer and orders, then ordered traversal of lineitem_order_postings and lineitem payloads in ascending o_orderkey to keep rowid and payload access near-sequential

data_structures.aggregation: sorted_group_by with per-order register accumulator + fixed_min_heap(10) per thread

indexes.purpose: enumerate BUILDING customer rowids without scanning customer
expand only BUILDING customers to candidate orders instead of scanning all orders
walk matching lineitem slices for qualifying orders after sorting them by order key

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category A plan mismatch. Iteration 2 still scans all 15M orders even though customer_segment_postings and orders_cust_postings can cut the order work down to the 300k BUILDING customers and roughly 1.46M surviving orders; iteration 3 showed that replacing this with a 15M-key dense order-state table is too expensive to build. The revised plan expands only BUILDING customers through orders_cust_postings, filters o_orderdate immediately, materializes a compact qualifying-order array that fits in LLC, sorts it by o_orderkey, and then probes lineitem_order_postings in that order so the rowid and payload accesses become streaming rather than scattered.
